name: Team Warner
subject: Virtual Town Hall for VA Students: 10/8 at 4:30 PM
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
Join Mark Warner for a discussion of the issues Virginia students face.
|
---

| |
Derek --

Senator Mark Warner is hosting a virtual town hall on **Thursday, October 8th at 4:30 PM ET** to answer questions from students across the Commonwealth about the issues they face during coronavirus and beyond. All are welcome to join! Here are the details: | **Facebook Live Town Hall with Senator Mark Warner**
Thursday, October 8th
4:30 PM ET
**[Click here to RSVP](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85da76/53d9897e/55b12b62/1c88eb2d/3494386468/VEsH/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.mobilize.us/vavictory/event/341504/")**
---
Mark will be joined by student leaders from across the Commonwealth and is excited to speak with them about their experiences with virtual learning, and other challenges they're facing.

Although this event is hosted on Facebook, you do **not** need a Facebook account to access the town hall. If you already have a question for Mark or any of our guests, you can respond directly to this email, or submit your question through Facebook during the event. They will get to as many questions as they can during the event.

We hope you'll join us! **[Click here to sign up.](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85da76/53d9897e/55b12b62/1c88eb2d/3494386468/VEsE/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.mobilize.us/vavictory/event/341504/")**

Thank you for your support.

-- Team Warner

name: =?utf-8?Q?Dani?=
subject: Help me protect our fields and families
body:
At a meet and greet this weekend, a couple approached me and shared their story. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/b2241c9ab8cc/help-me-protect-our-fields-and-families?e=140a9b81e7)
---
| |
---
| |
#
At a meet and greet this weekend, a couple approached me and shared their story. They're farmers with day jobs, as has been the case in their family for generations. Their land is a part of their identity and, though times and circumstances have changed, their commitment to ensuring that land is tended to hasn't.

"What are you going to do for farmers," the woman asked.

Here's what I'm going to do:
1 -- Fight for trade deals that don't perpetuate a trade war at the expense of farmers
2 -- Provide resources for farmers to be a part of the solution to the climate crisis (NOT hold them accountable to strict regulations without providing support to meet them)
3 -- Ensure subsidy dollars don't go to big, corporate farms who are eating up the family farms who make up this district

And that's just to start. Our current Representative has cozied up to one small farming operation and called himself a champion for Agriculture, but the farmers of IL-16 are my friends, family and neighbors.

**Farming is hard work**. We shouldn't make it harder. I'm running to protect our fields and families.

[Can you chip in today to help me do that?](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=6ecb672b92&e=140a9b81e7)

Thanks,
Dani

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Update: President Trump returns to the White House
body:
President Trump just announced that he will return to the White House from the Walter Reed National Military Medical Center in Maryland this evening. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=80b4bedb2f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • October 5, 2020
---
| | |
# **Update: President Trump returns to the White House**

**President Trump just announced** that he will return to the White House from the Walter Reed National Military Medical Center in Maryland this evening.

**“Feeling really good!** Don’t be afraid of Covid. Don’t let it dominate your life,” the President shared on Twitter. “We have developed, under the Trump Administration, some really great drugs & knowledge. I feel better than I did 20 years ago!”

**Early Friday morning,** President Trump announced that he and First Lady Melania Trump had tested positive for the Coronavirus. “We will get through this together,” he said.

**In a video** shared yesterday, the President updated Americans on his health and thanked the incredible team of doctors and nurses at Walter Reed Medical Center for their care.

**🎬** [**President Trump: “We’re getting great reports from the doctors.”**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=40cfe51a61&e=3ee4985eea)

**Out of an abundance of caution,** President Trump spent the weekend working from the Presidential suite at Walter Reed. He received state-of-the-art care from a team of physicians, and his condition has continued to improve quickly.

**The President also received** some of the therapies that the Trump Administration has championed through Operation Warp Speed, a government-led and nationwide effort to develop a Coronavirus vaccine and COVID-19 therapeutics quickly.

**A short while ago** , Physician to the President Dr. Sean Conley provided Americans with an update about President Trump’s condition and his return to the White House.

**“He has met or exceeded** all standard hospital discharge criteria,” Dr. Conley said. “The team and I agree that all our evaluations—and, most importantly, his clinical status—support the president's safe return home where he'll be surrounded by world-class medical care 24/7.”

**President Trump has continued** to work tirelessly for the American people while fighting this virus. “He spent most of the afternoon conducting business,” read a memorandum from Dr. Conley that he [**released Saturday evening**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8f64615c95&e=3ee4985eea)**.**

**First Lady Melania Trump** thanked Americans for their prayers and continued support. “I am feeling good & will continue to rest at home. Thank you to medical staff & caretakers everywhere, & my continued prayers for those who are ill or have a family member impacted by the virus,” [**she wrote on Twitter**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=28e65439b4&e=3ee4985eea)**.**
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day

|
---

---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Pat=20Hackett?=
subject: Celebrating teachers for their knowledge, support and inspiration
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pat Hackett for Congress
body:
My appreciation for educators is not contained to this one day: Education is one of my top priorities in my campaign for Congress. | | | | [ ](https://foundationbluemedia.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c156e612ce88db66e08a28197&id=f8f673e9dc&e=5886ca081d)
---
| | | Dear Friend,

Teaching is one of the most important professions there is, but it is also among the most difficult — especially now.

COVID-19 has caused teachers to face an unprecedented challenge as they’ve shifted from in-person teaching to new online classrooms. They have learned to teach a class with a mask on and have found a way for students to collaborate on projects while six feet away.

Please join me in celebrating [World Teachers’ Day](https://foundationbluemedia.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c156e612ce88db66e08a28197&id=03e916d5f3&e=5886ca081d).

My appreciation for educators is not contained to this one day: [Education is one of my top priorities](https://foundationbluemedia.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c156e612ce88db66e08a28197&id=49e0d9d4e4&e=5886ca081d) in my campaign for Congress.

[I believe that no matter your background, everyone deserves a fair shot.](https://foundationbluemedia.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c156e612ce88db66e08a28197&id=28a962f41e&e=5886ca081d) That starts with a strong education system -- from early childhood through college and the trades. I will also advocate for policies that incentivize good teachers and allow more innovation in the classroom.

On this World Teacher’s Day, I’d love to hear from you: What do you think needs to be done to help our schools and children? How can I support Hoosier teachers, students and families?

---
| [CHAT WITH PAT ABOUT EDUCATION](https://foundationbluemedia.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c156e612ce88db66e08a28197&id=fe09627fad&e=5886ca081d "CHAT WITH PAT ABOUT EDUCATION")
---
|
I have enjoyed my years spent in the classroom teaching law and theology. Now I look forward to the years ahead advocating on behalf of our public education system — but I can only do that with your help.

clean_body: My appreciation for educators is not contained to this one day: Education is one of my top priorities in my campaign for Congress. | | | | | | | Dear Friend, Teaching is one of the most important professions there is, but it is also among the most difficult — especially now. COVID-19 has caused teachers to face an unprecedented challenge as they’ve shifted from in-person teaching to new online classrooms. They have learned to teach a class with a mask on and have found a way for students to collaborate on projects while six feet away. Please join me in celebrating . My appreciation for educators is not contained to this one day: in my campaign for Congress. That starts with a strong education system -- from early childhood through college and the trades. I will also advocate for policies that incentivize good teachers and allow more innovation in the classroom. On this World Teacher’s Day, I’d love to hear from you: What do you think needs to be done to help our schools and children? How can I support Hoosier teachers, students and families? | "CHAT WITH PAT ABOUT EDUCATION" | I have enjoyed my years spent in the classroom teaching law and theology. Now I look forward to the years ahead advocating on behalf of our public education system — but I can only do that with your help. | "INVEST IN EDUCATION FOR $25" | "INVEST IN EDUCATION FOR $50" | "INVEST IN EDUCATION FOR $75" | "INVEST IN EDUCATION FOR $100" | "INVEST IN EDUCATION FOR ANOTHER AMOUNT" | If you’ve saved your information with ActBlue, your donation will go through automatically. Today, I thank all the teachers of IN02 for dedicating their careers to inspiring and empowering children. Pat | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Pat Hackett is a lifelong advocate for her community. Now, she's running for Congress to reclaim the People's House, return authority to the people, and end the corrupting influence of money in politics. She will fight to end corruption in politics so that we can provide healthcare for everyone, end the gun violence epidemic, and address the catastrophic challenges of climate change. | Pat Hackett for CongressPO Box 8066South Bend, IN 46660-8066 | | Paid for by Pat Hackett for Congress

Friend \- we have huge news!

_Associated Builders and Contractors of Indiana/Kentucky (ABC) just endorsed Governor Eric Holcomb’s campaign for a second term._
[**Join ABC and endorse Eric**](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=1fdfce766ecb47589e6c51a42699da67&_e=XzocKg8K0TQcmILJTly0B1ePCusB95jz9hHys097NimmUU65fu5qf5LoZRpeq2WnNQjd0Nlkv-NsHTmpsG7pq3TwZFhMWUj77bw5y98HoZtfzckKMUf2zgXqRt2t1bsLfLVfLqUSbyfWLVG8uCI4PaJ895OvFO0c544sV3dwjXAzrZdWhr_oEq0vrC0xirTf8MrvlNY1S1Q87Y97CC_18hdCyf2U7HtGuWVzLlGv8cjb77rUmypRfyp54FmmSU8na5FYSFUL2hWZBnKZps-3BtcYjg60AmU3GEej6ZpKQJVghkw_82I_cMP3NidWkGJsLDNZKUSnnJbhvCrKxnECl0DfoDZunabgy6XGkWKQxW0giQHlf_tGz6DtDeMaMzyh8ORiWGVCMuB-qKqG5cEigiyqPOsmNV92jKFNYkBWJBHMwQ_KNcGhnT4i--wsFNaVgjMJvO7bYKK1GcLu_d4-8gvMagIsOIllv9Vfb-shA_Q%3D)
Governor Holcomb’s dedication to keeping construction projects moving forward safely during the pandemic and his commitment to expanding workforce training options are major reasons why he earned ABC’s endorsement.

Indiana’s construction companies have played a key role in Indiana’s unemployment rate dropping to 6.4% (below the national rate and the rate of all neighboring states) and Eric is looking forward to continuing to work with them on building One Indiana for All (literally!).
[ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=1fdfce766ecb47589e6c51a42699da67&_e=XzocKg8K0TQcmILJTly0B1ePCusB95jz9hHys097NimmUU65fu5qf5LoZRpeq2WnNQjd0Nlkv-NsHTmpsG7pq3TwZFhMWUj77bw5y98HoZtfzckKMUf2zgXqRt2t1bsLfLVfLqUSbyfWLVG8uCI4PaJ895OvFO0c544sV3dwjXAzrZdWhr_oEq0vrC0xirTf8MrvlNY1S1Q87Y97CC_18hdCyf2U7HtGuWVzLlGv8cjb77rUmypRfyp54FmmSU8na5FYSFUL2hWZBnKZps-3BoqnbyNwuDBcNiX9z4COPQXsp3CIhfprNUsmip_xnKKc_UY1pBvVbqwSrmS55_0F2i1YbfZp4TbOXJuC7ZoeFt_ZMScrJ4wQ34-FER4zotu6BXljO30B-kWKcTh8-kdIAaHNIGcByoyVJTPCY8q4ZqssOjZ6zRkuWqnNV7gzHZHlyR2uwfA8wROGOi3UZeHx5hnzF2Oo7fJ9bTvGoU3j5NI%3D)
Here’s what the president of ABC had to say about Eric’s leadership:
_“Governor Holcomb’s leadership has helped Indiana’s construction industry continue to build Indiana’s economy, even through a global pandemic. Hoosier construction professionals are working safety on jobs across the state, while job training programs expanded by Governor Holcomb are helping more Hoosiers build the skills for great careers in construction. Governor Holcomb has built the environment for business success, and he’s the right person to keep it going for another four years.” - J.R. Gaylor, president of ABC _
ABC is made up of 515 companies across the state that employ more than 95,000 Hoosiers. The group offers construction education, training, and on-the-job apprenticeships.

Governor Holcomb has led the way in creating opportunities for Hoosiers to build skills for high-wage, in-demand jobs, including jobs in the construction industry, such as
* Launching and expanding Indiana’s Next Level Jobs program — including investing a total of $75 million in CARES Act funding to help Hoosiers impacted by COVID-19.
* Providing Workforce Ready Grants that have helped more than 10,000 Hoosiers receive free job training for high-wage, in-demand jobs. Since June, more than 5,500 Hoosiers have used these grants to enroll in classes or training.
* Providing Employer Training Grants that reimburse employers who train, hire, and retrain Hoosiers to fill in-demand positions. Since June, these grants have helped more than 7,300 employees to improve their skills.
* Serving on the Trump Administration American Workforce Policy Advisory Board.

[**Join ABC and endorse Eric**](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=1fdfce766ecb47589e6c51a42699da67&_e=XzocKg8K0TQcmILJTly0B1ePCusB95jz9hHys097NimmUU65fu5qf5LoZRpeq2WnNQjd0Nlkv-NsHTmpsG7pq3TwZFhMWUj77bw5y98HoZtfzckKMUf2zgXqRt2t1bsLfLVfLqUSbyfWLVG8uCI4PaJ895OvFO0c544sV3dwjXAzrZdWhr_oEq0vrC0xirTf8MrvlNY1S1Q87Y97CC_18hdCyf2U7HtGuWVzLlGv8cjb77rUmypRfyp54FmmSU8na5FYSFUL2hWZBnKZps-3BkYN_dVwdFHrYNTmE18mxNBlirLv-ay0zUvy0NKOB0TRlwigjTqQuggNTrEOosFchub_CoyIOTRgvqMgkLPbLuokd9INlgmkwwq7icIhggY1B2ylMeml4dyIDgqgO2XSoTOMzhLXNmXXVQSvF90bNEBhYs0rF7abL-lZLS3VQIv-Rt1JqIOGupEN4TKEBmWK0dUAccDd6_NbHRoqCkIiuhQ%3D)
The impact of Governor Holcomb’s proven workforce training strategies is extending beyond our state’s borders — he is working hand-in-hand with Ivanka Trump and Secretary of Commerce Wilbur Ross to strengthen America’s pipeline of skilled workers.

Here’s what Eric had to say about ABC’s endorsement:
_“Indiana’s economy is gearing back up, and that starts with supporting our job creators and making sure all Hoosiers have opportunities to thrive. ABC Indiana/Kentucky and their members across the state are key partners as we’re stepping on the gas to expand training opportunities and get all Hoosiers into great careers – including in construction.” - Governor Eric Holcomb_
Thank you for reading,

Holcomb Crew

name: MJ Hegar
subject: Protect the Affordable Care Act >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Did you see what Sen. Cornyn said about the Affordable Care Act and protecting people with pre-existing conditions, everyone?
[](https://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc55e/2a380d31/20c4e0ab/f0b4c9/74084535/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0aGVnYXIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDIwMTAwNV9NV0g0TUpIX25hIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCJ9/?g=YHsLejpTVzRXNXNZdVQ0tHA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4hegar?refcode=e20201005_MWH4MJH_na&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")
He thinks that gutting the Affordable Care Act is fine, even though millions of people with pre-existing conditions receive coverage through it.
**And just hours after this tweet, he voted in favor of overturning the ACA _again._**
> [Protect the ACA ](https://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc55e/2a380d31/20c4e0ab/f0b4c9/74084535/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0aGVnYXIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDIwMTAwNV9NV0g0TUpIX25hIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCJ9/?g=YHsLejpTVzRXNXNZdVQ0tHA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4hegar?refcode=e20201005_MWH4MJH_na&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")
Health care is deadly serious for millions of Texans, but Sen. Cornyn has voted REPEATEDLY to rip it away. He voted not to stop a lawsuit that seeks to overturn the Affordable Care Act -- in the midst of the deadliest pandemic in generations.
Here's the deal, y'all. There are at least 4.8 million people with pre-existing conditions in Texas, and now, anyone who's had COVID-19 will have a pre-existing condition as well. **The Affordable Care Act protects ALL of us by ensuring that insurance companies can 't charge us more for having a pre-existing condition.**
**Sen. Cornyn has been on the government payroll for nearly forty years. Between his benefits and three taxpayer-funded pensions, he just doesn 't understand just how vital the ACA is for millions of us.**
[**Help me defeat Sen. Cornyn, because I will always defend our health care >>**](https://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc55e/2a380d31/20c4e0ab/f0b4c9/74084535/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0aGVnYXIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDIwMTAwNV9NV0g0TUpIX25hIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCJ9/?g=YHsLejpTVzRXNXNZdVQ0tHA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4hegar?refcode=e20201005_MWH4MJH_na&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")
**Will you pitch in again to protect people with pre-existing conditions?**
